Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log with five cases We need to analyze the patterns and design a POWL model that captures the process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process starts with OR then MS then QS then AS This seems sequential and common to all cases

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After the initial AS there is sometimes an IQC step Sometimes IQC is skipped case 4 In cases 1 2 3 5 we see at least one IQC In cases 3 and 5 we see multiple IQC steps interleaved with AS

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So after the initial AS there is a pattern optionally there can be one or more IQC steps each possibly followed by another AS

Name: Validation - IND
Text: But note in case 3 we see two consecutive IQC steps without an AS in between Actually in case 3 AS then IQC then IQC then AS then IQC then AS then TST

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We can model the IQC and AS as a loop

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But note that in case 3 we have two IQC steps without an AS in between So if we model the loop as IQC AS then we cannot have two IQC in a row

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So we can model the block as a loop that allows zero or more iterations and each iteration consists of one or more IQC steps followed by an AS

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can model the iteration body as IQC plus AS meaning one or more IQC steps followed by an AS Then the entire block is IQC plus AS repeated zero or more times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In case 4 Block is empty so we go directly to TST So the segment after the first AS and before TST is zero or more repetitions of IQC plus AS

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: IQC plus equals loop over IQC with silent transition

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then for the Block we want zero or more repetitions of IQC plus AS

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: If we set A equals IQC plus and B equals AS then the loop always ends with A But we want the loop to end with AS So this does not work

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can define Body as a strict partial order with IQC plus before AS Then define a loop with Body and silent transition Then define Block as a choice between silent transition and that loop

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After TST we sometimes have RT re test which leads back to TST

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So we can model this as a loop TST loop equals loop with TST and RT

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then after the TST loop we have PK then optional LB then DP then D Because labeling LB may be skipped

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: LB choice equals exclusive choice between LB and silent transition

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the entire model OR then MS then QS then AS then Block then TST loop then PK then choice of LB or skip then DP then D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let us check with the cases Case 4 Block is skip zero Body then directly to TST loop Then TST no RT Then PK then LB then DP then D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Block is one Body IQC plus then AS Then TST no RT Then PK then no LB then DP then D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Block is one Body IQC plus then AS Then TST then RT then TST Then PK then LB then DP then D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Block is two Bodies first Body IQC plus with two IQC steps then AS second Body IQC plus with one IQC then AS Then TST then RT then TST

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Block is three Bodies each Body is IQC plus then AS Then TST then RT then TST then RT then TST

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This seems to match So the POWL model seems to capture the process
